verse_specific_anchors: jātasya hi dhruvo mṛtyuḥ (for one born, death is certain)
dhruvaṁ janma mṛtasya ca (for the dead, birth is certain) — the symmetric paired claim
the structural-inevitability argument distinct from the concessive-grant of 2.26
verdict_reason: The engineering analog is anchored on the symmetric birth↔death pairing specific to 2.27. The phrasing 'whatever ships has a deprecation ahead of it; whatever gets deprecated has a replacement ahead of that' directly mirrors jātasya... mṛtyuḥ / mṛtasya... janma. This would NOT apply to 2.26 (which is the concessive opening, not the closing), nor to 2.28 (which is the three-phase structural argument from beings, not the paired-cycle argument), nor to 2.20 (which asserts the imperishable rather than the cycle of replacement).
